content:
^
also as someone who is 5'3" and petite and is afab, I really don't like the implications there, friend
doll is an adult, even if she is short and petite. end of discussion, no?

I think it's a case of. . . it got rid of the *worst* of the dissolution but there's always technically glances from the supreme will so dissolution is never really *gone*, or something like that? that's how I read it at least, take this with a grain of salt
as far as we know dissolution is caused by the gaze(s) of the supreme will
iirc its the in-game timeline
i wanted to say this then didnt
Regarding Herbert, theres  wiggle room where they could have known each other before she was officially enrolled, or as you say, it could be another writing inconsistency

this is also how i interpreted it but to add: (spoiling jic) ||dissolution is always somewhat guaranteed because anything with a gnostic index 21+ will attract the gaze, which (specifically mentioned) even fish can have||
i reread that scene because i really liked it akshdudisgsof
nods nods
i think doll/herbert being familiar is more from encountering each other during herbert's many clashes with mythag rather than knowing each other as students

btw i finsihed arc 2 chap 2 and was just wondeirng
basically clem was a sickly human and her dad made her turn into a bug or smth right cuz the alien creatures wanted her?
but why was clems family ostracised from birth?
cuz there were issues even before the bug transformation stuff right
also wth is the committee that clem is investigating or keeping ties with
it’s 2026 and there is still people thinking short petite anime girls equals children
I wonder how many people actually understand that people are not that stupid and can distinguish fiction and reality
they didnt want clem specifically. the Insectoids would accept almost anyone indiscriminately
dont even get me started <:takenotes:1357186646294138890> no one's an adult until they grow past 5'4 <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
😭 guess i'm never going to be an adult then, i've been stuck at 5'3" and will likely never get taller
i was going to ask a lore question but forgot what i wanted to ask <:takenotes:1357186646294138890> the dissolution got me i guess
we got caught up in being short 🤝
nods nods

AH i remembered my question
so, in chapter 8 i believe it was mentioned juliette is apparently a newer leader of the lightbearers, or at least came into her position recently; do we know when salvador died, since he apparently mentored her? is she meant to be just a long-lived person who's one of many that gained leadership of the lightbearers, or is she an awakener who founded the cult, or some alternate third option?

juliette and the timeline surrounding her confuses me and i am shaking her like "please give me a concrete idea of your position in the timeline so i don't have to collapse into a puddle of tears trying to write fanfic about the bird twins"

iirc timeline is roughly: 
Before Flood (so more than 300 yrs ago): 
- salvador tries saving people from dissolution using his tablets, and mentors juliette
- salvador gets denounced as a heretic and crucified
- at some point juliette or someone else founds the lightbearer church (there's a mention of a specific family involved with the founding but we've gotten very little lore about the lightbearers in general)
-eventually salvador gets awakened sometime in the 200s AF
there's a lot of open unresolved questions with juliette and the lightbearer church in general

i see i see
so, evidence points towards juliette being some kind of immortal, then? maybe a transformed awakener or something? since otherwise i. . . have no idea how she would live that long

-shakes biav- please elaborate on juliette and the lightbearers. please
if there's unanswered questions i guess i'll just make my own answers! such is the way of life i suppose

side note i am once again asking if biav would perhaps be willing to share the cut content from the bird twins' arc. i wanna see how they ended up at mythag and how they settled in there. give us the gdoll event also. i wanna seeeeee
I thought clem was a || distant descendent of the insectoids when they were physically present on the world ||?
so like, not her specifically but
still something
i had assumed Juliette was under salv after his awakening, and then she split off to found the church
I might just be wrong I did the story at like 2am lol
but theres room to speculate that juli might be an awakener herself
mm, the archives says the church of the lightbearers was formed pre-flood, so if salvador was awakened after the flood then she'd not have been able to found the church i don't think
. . . juliette please don't be a timeline paradox
i think it makes more sense for it to be before flood since salvador getting crucified is a dramatic example of him being Too Nice that juliette would take issue with, but it could be either way
though the idea of juliette joining the evil church just because salvador is too nice in like. resolving a domestic dispute is really funny

i think it could be really interesting, and maybe a bit of a like narrative parallel maybe, if the person to found the church of the lightbearers *wasn't* juliette, but was still someone who'd been like a disicple of salvador or something and had a similar "he was too nice" belief
and then juliette was a disciple/apprentice of salvador after he'd been awakened only for her to be similarly dissilusioned and drawn into the church, eventually becoming the leader of it. something something history likes to rhyme
(plus it would match ch 8 saying she came into power *recently*, even if the bird twins' stories were technically the more recently-written bits of lore)
was it that they just made that village where clem came from insects?
oh also on how awakeners r formed
i originally thought that some ritual is needed like the awakening ceremony by mythag or the seafarers rituals to create an awakener
but can awakeners just like form after dying spontaenously? cuz how exaactly does kath just become an awakener
ah, that's where the base of the awakener juli theory comes from
kath is a special case since its heavily implied her Supreme Will directly intervened
yeah everyone in that village either left or became insects
thats why clems dad wanted her to leave before eventually inviting her back
theres more than one supreme will?
niko should have said 'higher will'
||there's the Supreme Will, Tohu, and then all awakeners are influenced by a higher will, with Kath's being Cthuga||
||The God vs gods||
ah every awakener has their higher will-? which is like a god in lovecraftion mythos
yeah
well, most
and then thats why awkaeners who have genesis versions r more close to their higher will
yeah
idk whats the proper term now, since gdoll uses Supreme Wills while the Genesis archive uses Higher Wills iorc
ahh ok so all the gods do actually exist
yeah just that we cant perceive them because theyre a higher plane of existence
i think in ch 8/9 they use ||the supreme will|| still
so i'm gonna assume its higher will for the others
cause it would be really confusing if they both use the same term
yeah i personally prefer Higher Wills too
i dont think i ever rmb seeing higher will
but maybe i forgot since i just assumed they went tgth
usually only gets brought up in flavour texts like awakener profile stories or the archive
also its implied, at least for some, that dissolution has fucked them up so bad they can't do much

||and presumably cthulhu||

content: he wrote a horror story based on __air conditioning__
He *what*
that is the take for Faint Tinct
they literally emitted space radiation
https://www.hplovecraft.com/writings/texts/fiction/ca.aspx
"Cool Air"
honestly his works reek with things he doesn’t understand being exaggerated
and mental illness
and racism
This is half incredibly neat and half insane.
this includes racism Niko
a guy who was afraid of everything he didnt know, not because it was eldritch, but because he wasn't very smart
Like on one hand, the idea of “Oh shit, this dude is an Ice Lich” is neat, but the whole thing is just him hating air conditioning.
he sounds like he needs a therapist
He’s *dead*
he would be afraid of therapists
Clem will be born sooner
clem is a character i could unironically see the old man himself writing about
and yes people still talking about him so why can’t I say anything
"My therapist is a bug and wants to turn me into a bug"
To be fair “The therapist is actively getting off on your trauma and may or may not try to exacerbate it” is a legitimately nightmarish idea.
Like that’s a realistically possible scenario.
its very telling that theres only one lovecraft story that features a woman and it still turns out to be her dad body swapped into her body
well he also wrote that one romance comedy
Girls are scarey they have cooties.
but on a more comedic note theres The Alchemist
lowkey he needed money

two fun facts about his fear of coldness though; Lovecraft apparently loved ice cream and, despite being set in Antarctica, At the Mountains of Madness pretty much never uses the cold conditions as a source of fear or even hindrance to the characters at all for whatever reason.
he wasn't afraid of the cold, he was afraid of __artificial__ cold
